raw_evidence: Download a free membership brochure and discover the benefits of joining the Manchester Digital community for your business The #MDTechCommunity was born out of a desire to facilitate collaboration and communication amongst Greater Manchester’s tech community Joining the #MDTechCommunity will connect you, immediately, with the brands, agencies, entrepreneurs, startups, freelancers, developers, marketers, investors, innovators anyone else that make up the tech and digital sector Whether you want to make useful connections, meet members of your tribe, discuss specific tech topics or just share information, the #MDTechCommunity is the place for you Find out other ways to connect with us on social

Download a free membership brochure and discover the benefits of joining the Manchester Digital community for your business Manchester Digital has launched its ‘Manifesto for the Northern Tech Economy’ in advance of the Greater Manchester Mayoral Elections on May 2 Whoever is elected Mayor of Greater Manchester will have the opportunity to supercharge the Manchester tech ecosystem - which is the fastest growing tech hub in Europe outside of London This is our call to arms for the Mayor of Greater Manchester to work alongside the tech industry and liaise with other stakeholders to create the right environment to grow our unique and brilliant tech ecosystem There is a vast scope to leverage digital technologies and innovations to drive economic growth, improve public services and enhance the quality of life for our local citizens

Connecting industry, academia, founders, investors and policymakers, the event will explore the infrastructure, talent and innovation needed to build the next generation of the intelligence economy Featuring keynote speakers, panel discussions, startup showcases and a private leadership dinner, Future Live will examine how the North can lead the next era of compute and innovation 📅 Date: Thursday 17th September 2026 🕒 Time: 10:00-17:30 📍 Location: Campfield 🎟️ Tickets Tickets are limited to maintain audience quality and seniority At Manchester Digital, we believe in creating an inclusive and diverse community where everyone feels welcome and valued. We are committed to ensuring that all our event attendees have a safe, enjoyable experience that meets their needs We invite all attendees to contact us if they have any dietary requirements, accessibility needs, or neurodiversity requirements, or if they require accommodations to respect their cultural or religious needs during their visit. We believe that by working together, we can create

You are welcome to present either a digital research poster or a lightning talk. If your research aligns with one of the Centre’s themes , please submit a sho Keynote 1: Jonathan Hall KC, 6kbw College Hill Jonathan Hall is a practising barrister at 6kbw College Hill in London where he specialises in national security, asset recovery, crypto and law enforcement powers, and has been a kc since 2014. He was appointed by the home secretary as the independent reviewer of terrorism legis Keynote 2: Dr. Emma L. Briant, University of Notre Dame Dr. Emma L. Briant is one of the world’s leading experts on information warfare and propaganda. Her research analyzing propaganda, media and technology spans nearly two decades during which time she has taken on some of the most rapidly evolving and disruptive political challenge

Motorsport has long been a proving ground for innovation, pushing the boundaries of performance, precision and speed. But the technologies, materials and manufacturing processes developed for the track increasingly have applications far beyond racing This Thursday Gathering explores how advances in motorsport are influencing sectors from aerospace and healthcare to sport, mobility and sustainable manufacturing. Bringing together manufacturers, engineers, researchers, startups and industry leaders, we'll examine the role of di Join us to discover where engineering excellence meets competitive advantage Powered by Advanced Research + Invention Agency

Take your AI knowledge from beginner to intermediate and learn how to implement it effectively across your organisation Many professionals understand AI's potential but are uncertain about how to apply it effectively in their business. This CPD-accredited workshop gives you the tools and knowledge to do so with confidence The evening begins with a presentation giving you a working understanding of how AI actually functions and breaking down the terminology. Food and refreshments are served while you take part in practical breakout sessions. Working in groups you will analyse and improve real busin No technical background is required. This workshop is designed for mid-to-senior professionals looking to lead or advise on AI adoption in their business This event is CPD-certified and carries 2.25 learning hours under the Practical Training Workshop category. Certificates will be issued at the end of the session

Join us for a special summer edition of the Thursday Gathering as we celebrate the final Digital Thursday and explore the technologies, ideas and people shaping the Intelligence Age This evening brings together researchers, founders, innovators and industry leaders to examine how AI is moving from the lab into the real world. We'll celebrate the communities, collaborations and entrepreneurial journeys that have helped shape the region's digital ecosystem, wh Expect inspiring conversations, new connections and a fitting send-off for Digital Thursday's remarkable journey

Your data's full of answers. The hard part is getting at them If you run your business across more than one system, you'll know the feeling. Your numbers are in your accounts, your customers are in your CRM, your sales and stock are somewhere else again. Getting a straight answer usually means exporting from each system and stitching it tog There's a better way, and it goes well beyond asking a quick question In this session, Craig Morrall, co-founder of Tugger, will show you how to get Claude doing real work with your business data. Not one-line answers, but full reports, dashboards, and reporting that runs itself Four things most people don't know Claude can do This is made for any business running on more than one system, whatever your sector. Already a Tugger customer? You'll pick up plenty to get more from what you've already got. New to us? You'll see exactly what's possible with your own data

When we say we’re Greater Manchester's business support organisation we really mean it. We work across all parts of the region, supporting businesses of all sizes, across all sectors. You could say we’re all in We’re the key to unleashing your business’s potential. That means helping you to think differently, attract new investment, reach new markets, upskill staff, and improve your environmental impact We’ll get under the skin of your business to really understand your vision. Then we’ll work with you to produce tangible outcomes – guiding, prompting, and doing some of the heavy lifting along the way. Making your ambitions become reality We don't just scratch the surface – we dive deep into the essence of your business to really get to grips with your ambitions. This enables us to deliver support that has the most impact. And we'll make sure every step we take together is a leap towards realising your goals Access the right funding options for you, from investment options to loans and grants Overcoming your challenges and finding a clear pathway to new commercial opportunities

We understand that every business is different, with its own set of unique characteristics and requirements. That’s why our tailored services are designed to meet your specific needs. It’s a personalised approach that allows us to deliver targeted, actionable solutions to improve You might need access to finance or help with getting your route-to-market strategy right. Maybe you want to develop your leadership skills or bring in specialist knowledge to unblock R&D challenges. You may not actually know what area of business improvement to target first One thing’s for sure – whatever your circumstances, we have the expertise, experience and contacts to help you Finding the right funding, and accessing finance and financial planning support Overcoming your challenges and finding a clear pathway to new commercial opportunities Supporting your journey to net zero and helping to drive the green economy

From hands-on training programmes, workshops and one-to-one support, to identifying funding and collaboration opportunities, we’re here for you to make sure you take full advantage of what these thriving markets offer And all this is provided free of charge to our clients, thanks to our funders and partners As Greater Manchester’s business support organisation, we exist to make life better for communities here – and we recognise that growing these four sectors will not only create the jobs of the future, but also address some of society’s biggest challenges We don’t just support businesses in these sectors, our advisors help businesses of all shapes and sizes- whether you're a hairdresser, a construction company or anything in between. If you’re looking for support, but not sure where to start, get in touch and we’ll point you in th Just before the national lockdown, Pippa Nixon transitioned from working in an established PR agency to becoming a freelance consultant. As her client base grew, she made the shift towards an agency model in 2021. Scoop PR was named the New Agency of The Year at the PR Moment Nor Insights, news, events and opportunities straight to your inbox

Our experts will support you with whatever your priorities are, whether that’s securing funding, becoming more innovative, developing yourself or your people, market expansion, improving your sustainability or social value... or any number of other challenges Likewise, if you’re not sure what to focus on, we can help you identify areas for improvement, potential growth opportunities, and any issues that might be impacting performance It’s our job to make your business better. Wherever you are in Greater Manchester, we’re here for you Wigan Rochdale Oldham Bolton MCR Trafford Bury Salford Tameside Stockport Just before the national lockdown, Pippa Nixon transitioned from working in an established PR agency to becoming a freelance consultant. As her client base grew, she made the shift towards an agency model in 2021. We provide tailored support through our EnterprisingYou programme to help those with a budding business idea, as well as established self-employed individuals, freelancers and micro businesses with up to 5 employees in Greater Manchester Learn ways to kickstart or improve your business, and reach your goals quicker and more efficiently. With workshops and free advice on a variety of topics, from registering your business, personal skills to marketing and business/personal finance – we’ve got you covered If you're prepared to take the next step and transform your passion into a livelihood, or if you've already moved into self-employment and are eager to go even further, you don't have to do it alone For those thinking of starting a business, ready to start a business, or wanting to grow an existing business Places are strictly limited, so register now to avoid disappointment. New sessions will be added regularly, keep checking this page for updates The EnterprisingYou programme is free of charge. It’s a government-funded project commissioned by Greater Manchester Combined Authority and delivered by GM Business Growth Hub. As part of the programme, you will be expected to commit and take part in the evaluation process

Supporting many of our clients for several years, it’s our aim to accompany businesses at every step of their growth journey. This includes expert guidance across all manner of opportunities and challenges, as well as providing networking opportunities, and making connections wit Our BGH Match events visit Bolton, Bury, Manchester, Oldham, Rochdale, Salford, Stockport, Tameside, Trafford and Wigan to ensure that the opportunity is accessible to all. Working in collaboration with local stakeholders and partners, we deliver BGH Match events to provide atten Connect with your local business community and like-minded professionals Stay up-to-date with everything that's going on in your local area Meet our GM Business Growth Hub team and discover more about the fully funded business support available for you and your organisation Your chance to establish connections, make introductions and create mutually beneficial partnerships and collaborations

BGH Match is the GM Business Growth Hub’s free business connector network, providing opportunities for businesses across Greater Manchester BGH Match is the GM Business Growth Hub’s free business connector network, providing opportunities for businesses across Greater Manchester to network and find out about the fully funded business support available to them Businesses from across Greater Manchester are invited to Venue @ Cornerstone for a morning of creating new contacts, encouraging collaboration and opening the door on future opportunities We like to keep things simple - our events are focused on what matters most: the people. That means plenty of time dedicated purely to networking, with minimal formalities. Grab a coffee, introduce yourself, and make the morning count Representatives from GM Business Growth Hub and Stockport Council will be on hand to explore the bespoke business support available to your business too 9:30am - 10:15am - Arrival, networking and registration

How to apply Lean Principles. How to implement standardised work. How to deliver real change The Lean Champion exhibits the learning behaviours and possesses the appropriate tools and skills to enable positive change. It is the role of the Lean Champion to coach others, so these skills and behaviours become regular habits and routines throughout the organisation. In this As this workshop is fully-funded, businesses are subject to funders' eligibility criteria. If your place is confirmed, you will receive an email from us with further details Insights, news, events and opportunities straight to your inbox Lee House 90 Great Bridgewater Street Manchester M1 5JW T: 0161 359 3050 E: bgh@growthco.uk

Undergraduate study is a significant financial investment, which is why we offer one of the most supportive financial packages out of all UK universities When calculating the cost of coming to university, you will need to consider two main elements - tuition fees and living costs while you study The information you submit on your application to study at the University will be used to determine your tuition fee status We have provided guidance on what you need to know about additional finance and living costs but you can also find further information on the government's dedicated website for student finance We have provided information for international students to plan their finances, from university fees and scholarships to guidance on the living costs they can expect in Manchester Find out more about international student finance

Find information on master's tuition fees and studying costs, as well as funding and scholarships available to help you plan your finances Your master’s fees will cover the cost of your study at the University, as well as registration, tuition, supervision, examinations and graduation You'll also have membership of The University of Manchester Library, the Students' Union and the Athletic Union Like other universities, we charge different fees depending on whether you’re classed as 'Home' or 'Overseas' for tuition fee purposes. Detailed information on how we assess your tuition fee can be found on our Your tuition fee status page Find information about the cost of living and accommodation in Manchester, as well as detailed breakdowns of living costs, on our postgraduate cost of living page There is less funding available for master’s courses than for undergraduate degrees or PhD programmes, so you should begin looking as soon as you can

An open day at The University of Manchester is the perfect way to come and get a feel for life at one of the UK’s largest single-site universities Find out more about the student experience in Manchester by coming to meet us on campus Learn more about our open day for master's and other postgraduate taught courses Visit us to get a taste of our learning environment and research culture

Whether you’re a multinational corporation, an ambitious SME or a forward-thinking social enterprise, discover the value working with our university can bring Times Higher Education Sustainability Impact Rankings 2026 "Manchester is a city at the forefront of technical and scientific innovation and social progress. Our University sits at the heart of this and, through our partnerships and collaborations, we are committed to fostering economic growth, social development and cultural enrichment. Dr Louise Bates, Director of Business Engagement and Knowledge Exchange Browse our case studies to learn more about our partnerships with organisations around the world, and discover how we tackle global challenges through interdisciplinary research and collaboration The Partnership Excellence Awards celebrate outstanding collaborations, partnerships and knowledge exchange activity that deliver impact across The University of Manchester and beyond

The University’s dedicated technology transfer organisation, responsible for transforming our world-leading research and ideas into commercial opportunities that deliver real-world impact through spinout, licensing and investment The Innovation Factory identifies, protects and develops intellectual property (IP) arising from the University’s research, guiding the journey from initial discovery to market-ready innovation. Its mission is to ensure that the breakthroughs, inventions and insights generated by Recognised as one of the UK’s leading university technology transfer offices, the Innovation Factory plays a vital role in supporting spinouts, licensing technology and securing investment, ensuring the University’s research excellence translates into commercial and societal bene Visit the Innovation Factory website to learn more We support University academics, researchers and postgraduate students to assess ideas, protect IP and explore commercial routes including spinouts and licensing. Our team works alongside researchers at every stage, offering expert advice and practical support to help turn innova Whether you’re a multinational corporation, an ambitious SME or a forward-thinking social enterprise, discover the value working with our university can bring

Business Support Services Business Support Services Business Support and Growth Services in Greater Manchester If you're a Greater Manchester business looking to scale, secure funding or turn a good idea into commercial growth, our Membership business support and growth services Your membership unlocks a range of business support services designed to improve operational efficiency, control costs, minimise risk, and supporting sustainable business development . Whether you need expert advice, reassurance on compliance, or practical help to strengthen your Our network of trusted experts supports every key function, including HR, Finance, Operations, and Management. From innovative UK business growth strategies to practical advice for scaling your company, discover how our business support services can maximise the value of your mem Access support for HR, Legal, Tax, VAT, Health and Safety via our complementary 360 support service. With a 24/7 advice line, and a comprehensive document library. Use as your first line of support, or your second opinion. Just know you're covered Energy costs have been rising and volatile for some time, Chamber Utilities can review your current energy bills and usage. The specialist team can either give you peace of mind that you’re on the best rate in the market, or they can try and bring your costs down We work directly with the AA to offer discounted fleet cover through Chamber membership. The discount applied can be up to 63%, with one member saving over £1,700 per year, just by being a member of our Chamber

Policy & Campaigns Policy & Campaigns Greater Manchester Chamber has been representing businesses for over 230 years, and counting We work with our members to campaign on a wide range of policy issues that affect businesses, their workforce and the wider communities in Greater Manchester. As a leading, independent, representation body, it is vital that we accurately represent the collective voice of business Following the 2024 General Election, the Labour government set out a series of measures outlining its plans for growth - including a new Industrial Strategy, new proposals for employment laws and more devolved powers. This is coupled with the Integrated Settlement awarded through There are a number of different routes to feed into policy and campaign work, including Chamber Assembly, our Campaign for Business, the GM Local Skills Improvement Plan and more Email Submit Campaign Activity Campaign for Business Formulated in collaboration with our members, Campaign for Business addresses the key issues that impact members and includes a set of recommendations for local, regional and central government that aim to make Greater Manchest The aim of the LSIP is to identify the skills and recruitment challenges affecting businesses in all sectors and locations in GM, and re-align training and current provision so that it meets the needs of employers

Export Documentation Export Documentation In the past three years we have helped traders move shipments worth over £1.8bn to the local economy. If you’re importing or exporting goods or services, then you’ll need export documentation to go with it, and we’re the largest provider We also offer legalisation, apostille & notarisation services, liaising with foreign embassies and the The Foreign Commonwealth & Development Office (FCDO) on your behalf. Leave the paperwork to us and focus on what really matters to grow your business With standard, express and priority services - we can make sure you get the documents you need, when you need them. Members of the Chamber can get up to 50% off selected documents Please bookmark this page for easy access to our Documentation Services price lists. You can find our main price list and a separate price-list for Arab Documentation and Legalisation services An ATA Carnet is used to temporarily import or export goods, without facing customs or value added tax charges. Commonly called the 'passport for goods' and can replace the needs for customs documents A UK Certificate of Origin is a document which states where the goods being shipped have been grown, manufactured or produced. This document will confirm the goods can legally enter a country and/or what relief in duty they might be eligible for

Enhanced Trade Partnership Enhanced Trade Partnership Doing business overseas can seem like a challenge, but it doesn't have to be. Whether you're experienced with trading internationally, or you're just starting to explore new markets, this partnership can provide the support an We offer one-to-one advice, introductions to global connections and events to help you learn, network and discover new opportunities Letters of Credit - July Thursday 9 Jul 9:30am - 12:30pm #internationaltrade Unlocking Opportunities with City of Osaka Thursday 9 Jul 9:30am - 5pm #internationaltrade Methods of Payment for International Trade - September Thursday 10 Sep 9:30am - 12:30pm Key Stakeholders GMCA Scale Globally Find out more about the Enhanced Trade Partnership, and see how the Greater Manchester partners can help you take your trade global

Campaign for Business Campaign for Business Campaign for Business is the Chamber’s blueprint for business growth in Greater Manchester. Originally launched ahead of the 2024 Mayoral Election and subsequently updated ahead of the 2024 General Election, Campaign for Business was sh Emma Holt, Chamber President, said: “Our Campaign for Business document is a great summary of the key issues facing businesses at present as well as setting out practical ways that these can be addressed. Whilst some of these can only, realistically, be resolved at a national lev “We also look at the potential from new initiatives coming over the next year or so around the GM Investment Zone, Atom Valley and ID Manchester and highlight ways that the benefits from innovation can be spread widely across all GM not just those areas and businesses nearest the “This campaign will evolve throughout the year to reflect current issues, progress and new challenges as they arise and we intend on working closely with businesses to make sure that we reflect their views accurately to the key decision makers at a local or national level.” Many GM businesses are struggling to enter export markets and/or expand their current exports - this trend is due to the continuing impact of Brexit and must be addressed as a priority. We need to alleviate the heavy compliance, administrative burden and the increased costs assoc When it comes to infrastructure projects, too often in recent years, the wrong decisions have been made for the wrong reasons, and we have gone backwards in relation to other countries. It is vital that the movement of goods, people and data is made easier, more sustainable and r

Events Northern Sustainability Summit 2026 Northern Sustainability Summit 2026 Official Chamber Post FREE Manchester Central, Windmill Street Manchester, Manchester, M2 3GX Friday 3 Jul 9am - 4:30pm Book Now The leading sustainability event in the North, for the curious and the e The Northern Sustainability Summit is back for 2026! It will bring together hundreds of business leaders from all sectors, from those leading on sustainability to those who are in the early stages of their journey Hosted at the iconic Manchester Central, last year’s event included over 40 speakers, 45 exhibitors, four workshops and over 1000 registered delegates The focus for 2026 is a return to Elevate's central belief: that business can, and should, be a force for good. We will explore how to blend profits with purpose, and how we can better collaborate to meet both sustainability and commercial targets The main stage content (running in the morning) will be a mix of panels, keynotes at lightning talks. The afternoon will several breakout workshops. Topics will include skills, finance, tech, property, tourism and social impact The exhibition hall includes our “incubator within an incubator” competition winners (applications open in February), sponsors and partners

Events Business Women's Network - July Business Women's Network - July Official Chamber Post £40.00 or £60.00 for non-members Junction, Manchester Central Convention Centre, Windmill Street Manchester, Manchester, M2 3GX Tuesday 7 Jul 12pm - 2:30pm Book Now Harnessing the Power o Don't forget, this isn't an event just for women - it's to promote equality, so allies and supporters are welcome too! We'll be welcoming to the event Kay Williams and Helen Roberts co-founders of the Talented Women Programme The event includes a two-course lunch and a drink We are open, inclusive and welcoming, and we celebrate the diversity which makes up our city region. We ask that all delegates and sponsors uphold our professional code at all times. We'll also be taking photos on the day, so please flag to us if you don't want to be featured

Events Summer Assembly Summer Assembly Official Chamber Post FREE Junction, Manchester Central Convention Centre, Windmill Street Manchester, Manchester, M2 3GX Wednesday 8 Jul 8:10am - 10:30am Sponsored By Adfin Book Now Join us at our Summer Assembly and be part of the conversa This is your opportunity to get the latest updates on skills, policy and the economy, discuss the challenges affecting business confidence, investment and growth, and ensure your voice is heard on the issues that matter most You will hear from President Emma Holt (who will be the Chamber's new CEO from 16th July), Chair Wayne Jones OBE and Subrahmaniam Krishnan-Harihara, Director of Business Policy & Research The Chamber Assembly plays a vital role in influencing regional policy and priorities. By sharing your insights and experiences, you'll help strengthen the collective voice of Greater Manchester's business community and contribute to future campaigns and initiatives Alongside valuable networking opportunities with fellow members and business leaders, you'll have the chance to feed directly into discussions that can drive positive change across our region Attendees will also be able to meet some of the team from our sponsor Adfin, who are visiting businesses and accountants across the UK to discuss one of the most common business challenges: getting paid on time

Join the Centre for Digital Trust and Society for an engaging day of talks and discussions!

Exploring Misinformation, Disinformation & Online Social Influence

Now in its 4th year, the Centre for Digital Trust and Society Annual Forum brings together leading voices from academia, government and civil society to explore the evolving challenges and opportunities shaping digital trust and security.

The Centre for Digital Trust and Society (CDTS) brings together interdisciplinary expertise from across the University of Manchester to examine the barriers and enablers of trust in digital technologies. Building on the success of our previous forums, this year’s event once again convenes national and international partners across our research clusters to reflect on emerging risks, share insights, and co‑create an inclusive vision for a safer digital future.

What to Expect

  • Two keynote talks from distinguished experts working at the forefront of digital trust, online influence, and information integrity.

  • Two thematic panels addressing the complex sociotechnical forces shaping today’s information environment.

  • Lightning Talks from University of Manchester PhD & ECR community on trust and security issues in digital, government, industry, and civil society contexts.

Who Should Attend
raw_evidence: The Centre for Digital Trust and Society (CDTS) brings together interdisciplinary expertise from across the University of Manchester to examine the barriers and enablers of trust in digital technologies. Building on the success of our previous forums, this year’s event once again Two keynote talks from distinguished experts working at the forefront of digital trust, online influence, and information integrity Two thematic panels addressing the complex sociotechnical forces shaping today’s information environment Lightning Talks from University of Manchester PhD & ECR community on trust and security issues in digital, government, industry, and civil society contexts Anyone with an interest in digital trust, online safety, and information integrity We warmly invite PhD students and early‑career researchers to showcase their work at our annual sell‑out forum on 2 July. You are welcome to present either a digital research poster or a lightning talk. If your research aligns with one of the Centre’s themes, please submit a shor

Motorsport has long been a proving ground for innovation, pushing the boundaries of performance, precision and speed. But the technologies, materials and manufacturing processes developed for the track increasingly have applications far beyond racing.

This Thursday Gathering explores how advances in motorsport are influencing sectors from aerospace and healthcare to sport, mobility and sustainable manufacturing. Bringing together manufacturers, engineers, researchers, startups and industry leaders, we'll examine the role of digital technologies, advanced materials, rapid prototyping and data-driven performance in shaping the next generation of products and industries.

Join us to discover where engineering excellence meets competitive advantage.

Join us for a special summer edition of the Thursday Gathering as we celebrate the final Digital Thursday and explore the technologies, ideas and people shaping the Intelligence Age.

This evening brings together researchers, founders, innovators and industry leaders to examine how AI is moving from the lab into the real world. We'll celebrate the communities, collaborations and entrepreneurial journeys that have helped shape the region's digital ecosystem, while looking ahead to the opportunities AI presents for business, society and the future of work.

Expect inspiring conversations, new connections and a fitting send-off for Digital Thursday's remarkable journey.

In partnership with digialThursday

An executive innovation experience, connecting AI, data and applications to real business outcomes.

Unlocking New Possibilities with the Oracle AI Ecosystem

Join us at Oracle's offices in Moorgate, London for Kovaion Connect 2026 - a focused executive briefing showing how Oracle Applications, Data, AI, OCI and Oracle APEX come together to help organisations innovate faster, operate smarter and unlock measurable business outcomes.

This is a half-day session built for senior decision-makers, combining thought leadership, the latest Oracle AI product updates, live demonstrations and one-to-one advisory conversations. The morning briefing moves from strategy to live innovation, followed by a networking lunch and optional private advisory slots in the afternoon. One Oracle. More value. Limitless innovation.

What you can expect

  • Executive insights into how AI, data and enterprise applications are reshaping the way organisations operate

  • The newest embedded AI capabilities across Oracle Fusion Applications

  • Release highlights and AI enhancements in Oracle APEX that accelerate enterprise innovation

  • Live demos of intelligent enterprise applications built on Oracle APEX and Oracle AI

  • A practical view of building an AI-ready data foundation with unified analytics

  • Private 1:1 executive advisory discussions with Oracle and Kovaion experts (afternoon, by request)

  • A networking lunch to exchange perspectives with peers and specialists

The day at a glance

  • 09:30 – 13:30 - Main session and networking lunch

  • 13:30 – 17:00 - Optional 1:1 executive advisory sessions

Who should attend

This briefing is designed for CIOs and CTOs, Heads of Applications, IT and Operations, Data and AI leaders, and Transformation heads - the people setting the enterprise technology, AI and digital strategy agenda.

Featured speakers include

  • Simon Hunt - Head of Product Management, Oracle APEX, Oracle UK

  • Mohana Priya Venkatachalam - Chief Projects Officer, Kovaion UK

  • Srinivas Nadella - Strategic Accounts Manager, Kovaion UK

With more Oracle and industry speakers to be announced.

You can view full agenda here - https:/www.kovaion.com/kovaion-connect-2026/

Venue: Oracle Corporation UK Ltd., One South Place, London EC2M 2RB - minutes from Moorgate, Liverpool Street and Bank stations, with rail, Underground and Elizabeth line connections.

At its heart, Venture Café is about connecting innovators and helping ideas move forward.

This Thursday Gathering puts that mission centre stage, with an evening of interactive workshops, peer learning and meaningful conversations. Rather than focusing on a single theme, we're creating space for the community to come together, share expertise, develop new skills and build connections across sectors and disciplines.

Come curious, leave inspired and take away practical insights, new contacts and fresh opportunities to help you move your next idea, project or venture forward.

Powered by Advanced Research + Invention Agency (ARIA)

Join us on the terrace at Neo for a relaxed social evening with drinks and nibbles in our beautiful mezzanine lounge.

Female Founders North brings together digital and tech founders from across the region in a safe, supportive environment to share stories, experiences, and build meaningful connections through informal networking.

This is your opportunity to:

  • Expand your tech and digital network

  • Discuss barriers, challenges, and opportunities with fellow entrepreneurs

  • Connect with founders who have navigated similar journeys or are currently on their entrepreneurial path

  • Be part of a thriving community that's been running successfully in Leeds for over two years and recently expanded to Manchester

Bruntwood SciTech has successfully delivered Female Founders Incubators across Leeds, Manchester, and Alderley Park, helping startups grow and supporting the next generation of women-owned tech businesses.

This event is designed for:

  • Female Founders and Female Entrepreneurs

  • Female CEOs and Directors at similar levels

Allies who can support women on their entrepreneurial journey are very welcome

If you are a consultant or intermediary, please explain why you wish to attend in the additional comments box when applying for your ticket.

  • Due to venue capacity, we can only accept one person per company

  • This is a quarterly event, so don't miss this opportunity to connect with your community

Ready to expand your network and be inspired by fellow female founders

Customers are no longer relying solely on Google. AI-powered search experiences such as ChatGPT, Gemini and Perplexity are changing how people discover products, services and suppliers, while Google's own search experience continues to evolve So what does this mean for your business? Should you still be investing in SEO? Are Google Ads delivering the same value? And how can you ensure your business is visible in an AI-first world? Join us for this practical member webinar with Jack Turner , Founder of etta , as we explore what marketing leaders, founders and business owners need to know to stay visible and competitive Whether you're reviewing your marketing strategy, investing in SEO, or simply trying to understand how AI is changing customer behaviour, you'll leave with practical insights and clear actions to help your business stay visible Jack Turner is the founder of etta, a digital consultant and Fractional Head of Digital specialising in ecommerce strategy and growth. With more than nine years' experience helping fashion and streetwear brands grow, Jack brings senior-level commercial thinking without the agency This webinar is exclusively available to Manchester Digital members and is free to attend
